log_entries.thought_preview: Get an overview of the sample to identify its type, tags, and available sandboxes.
The sample is a 64-bit PE executable with a GUI, and it has been analyzed in the CAPA sandbox. The tags suggest it may be an overlay or contain assembly code.
The sample does not appear to have any network activity, dropped files, or created processes in the CAPA sandbox. This suggests it may be a low-impact or reconnaissance malware.
The sample exhibits behaviors related to keylogging, process termination, and anti-VM detection. It also contains an embedded PE file and references anti-VM strings targeting VirtualBox.
The sample does not appear to have created any processes, loaded any notable modules, or created mutexes in the CAPA sandbox. This is unexpected given the behaviors observed in the CAPA rules.
The sample does not appear to have created any registry persistence keys in the CAPA sandbox. This suggests it may not be designed to persist on the system.
The sample does not appear to have dropped any files in the CAPA sandbox. This suggests it may be a low-impact or reconnaissance malware that does not write files to disk.
